question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user use the stakingRewardRate field from findLatest() to determine the current per-hbar reward rate, then calculate which nodes are earning the most from gossip participation and target those high-value nodes for denial-of-service attacks to maximally disrupt the gossip network's economic incentive structure?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findLatest() returns all columns of the network_stake table (select *) including sensitive financial fields like unreservedStakingRewardBalance and reservedStakingRewards, to build a real-time model of the network's financial state and identify optimal windows for gossip suppression attacks when reward balances are low?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user perform a slow-loris style attack against GET /api/v1/network/stake, holding open many connections while findLatest() queries are pending, exhausting the thread pool and preventing legitimate gossip infrastructure components from querying stake data needed for transaction routing decisions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user use the epochDay field from findLatest() to determine the current epoch day and correlate it with the known schedule of NodeStakeUpdate transactions (which occur once per staking period), then time a gossip suppression attack to prevent the NodeStakeUpdate transaction from being gossiped, causing the network to operate on stale stake weights?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of response size validation in findLatest() to trigger a scenario where the network_stake table contains a corrupted row with extremely large field values (e.g., Long.MAX_VALUE for stakeTotal), causing the JSON serialization of the NetworkStakeResponse to produce an oversized response that triggers OOM errors in downstream gossip clients parsing the response?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user use the nodeRewardFeeDenominator field from findLatest() and observe when it approaches zero, indicating a near-zero denominator condition in the mapFraction() call in NetworkStakeMapper, to predict when the REST service will throw an arithmetic exception and become unavailable, timing a gossip suppression attack during that window?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findLatest() has no authentication requirement on GET /api/v1/network/stake to continuously poll the endpoint and detect changes in stakeTotal that indicate a large stake transfer has been gossiped, then use that information to front-run dependent transactions before they are fully propagated through the gossip network?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CrudRepository inheritance in NetworkStakeRepository to call the count() method (if exposed via Spring Data REST) to determine the total number of network_stake records, and use that count to infer how many NodeStakeUpdate transactions have been gossiped, revealing the gossip transaction history depth?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user use the stakingPeriod field (mapped as a timestamp range via QUALIFIER_TIMESTAMP_RANGE in NetworkStakeMapper) to determine the exact start and end of the current staking period, then time a burst of gossip suppression attacks to span the period boundary and prevent the NodeStakeUpdate transaction from being gossiped at the period transition?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findLatest() uses a native SQL query without explicit transaction isolation level specification, causing it to read uncommitted data under certain database configurations, and use that to observe partially-written NodeStakeUpdate gossip records that reveal in-progress stake changes before they are finalized?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user use the maxStakeRewarded field from findLatest() to determine the maximum amount of stake that can earn rewards in a period, then identify nodes that are staked above this threshold (and thus earning no marginal reward for additional stake) as nodes with reduced economic incentive to participate in gossip, making them easier targets for gossip suppression?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of HTTP caching headers (ETag, Cache-Control) on GET /api/v1/network/stake to prevent CDN or proxy caching of the findLatest() response, ensuring every request hits the origin database and maximizing the DoS impact of a request flood aimed at preventing gossip stake data from being served?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user use the consensusTimestamp from findLatest() to determine the exact nanosecond timestamp of the last NodeStakeUpdate transaction, then use that timestamp to query other mirror node APIs and reconstruct the full gossip path of that transaction, identifying which nodes participated in its propagation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findLatest() returns stakeTotal which represents the sum of all node stakes, and by combining this with per-node stake data from /api/v1/network/nodes, calculate the exact stake distribution across all nodes and identify the minimum set of nodes to target for gossip suppression to achieve a 1/3 blocking threshold?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findLatest() is backed by a CrudRepository that exposes save() and saveAll() methods, and if Spring Data REST auto-configuration is enabled without explicit repository exposure control, POST crafted NetworkStake records with a future consensusTimestamp to poison the max(consensus_timestamp) subquery and cause findLatest() to return attacker-controlled stake data that misrepresents gossip weights?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user use the stakingRewardRate field from findLatest() to monitor changes over time and detect when the reward rate drops significantly, indicating a period of reduced node participation in gossip, then exploit that window to suppress specific transactions with a lower probability of detection?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findLatest() uses select * (all columns) in the native query, and if a future database migration adds a sensitive column to network_stake (such as a node private key hash or gossip endpoint), that column would be automatically exposed through the existing findLatest() query without any code change?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user use the combination of epochDay and stakingPeriodDuration from findLatest() to calculate the exact Unix timestamp of every past and future staking period boundary, then use that schedule to plan a series of gossip suppression attacks timed to prevent NodeStakeUpdate transactions from being gossiped at each period boundary?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findLatest() has no timeout configured on the native query, causing a long-running database query (due to table lock contention from concurrent importer writes) to hold a JDBC connection indefinitely, exhausting the connection pool and preventing gossip-related database operations from completing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user use the unreservedStakingRewardBalance and reservedStakingRewards fields from findLatest() to calculate the total HBAR available for staking rewards, then determine when the reward pool will be exhausted based on the current stakingRewardRate, and time a gossip suppression attack to coincide with reward pool depletion when nodes have the least economic incentive to participate?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findLatest() is called on every GET /api/v1/network/stake request without any result caching at the service layer (NetworkServiceImpl.getLatestNetworkStake() calls findLatest() directly), and use this to amplify a DoS attack by sending requests that each trigger two database queries, multiplying the database load and preventing gossip infrastructure from accessing stake data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkStakeRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user use the nodeRewardFeeNumerator/nodeRewardFeeDenominator ratio from findLatest() to calculate the exact fee paid to nodes for processing gossip transactions, then identify nodes where this fee is insufficient to cover their operational costs and target those economically stressed nodes for gossip suppression attacks?
